one_time_description: COLA payments of $12,133,572 in 2021 vs $1,974,455 in 2020 — likely a one-time cost of living allowance distribution to members
economic_notes: Nation has significant LNG-related business entities including Cedar LNG Export Development Ltd and multiple LNG limited partnerships and joint ventures. Kitamaat LNG investments total ~$65.9M book value. Investment in business entities includes commercial real estate and LNG LPs/JVs with total assets of $81.4M. Nation transferred 17 LPs and JVs to HN Projects Limited Partnership during the year. COVID-19 impacts noted.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) Social Housing TCA amortized at CMHC-required rate rather than useful life as per PSAS; (2) auditors unable to obtain audited financials for business entities so investment in business entities is unverified. Council remuneration of $415,192 disclosed in expenses by type but no individual schedule provided. Large COLA payments ($12.1M vs $1.9M prior year) are unusual. Nation holds very large investment portfolio (~$198M book value) including Kitamaat LNG investments and Legacy Trust. Accumulated surplus of $280.5M reflects significant investment holdings. Total government transfers calculated as ISC ($8,359,003) + FNHA ($2,686,613) + DFO ($409,876) + Province of BC ($2,563,384) + CMHC ($170,522) = $14,189,398. Haisla Minors LNG Trust Fund excluded from financial statements as assets held in trust for third parties. Net financial assets not explicitly stated on face of statements.
